title: Display all prices in Indian Rupees (₹) across the frontend

requirements.summary: Update the price formatting utility to display all prices in Indian Rupees (₹) with Indian number formatting, ensuring the change cascades to every component that displays prices
acceptanceCriteria: The formatPrice (or equivalent) utility in utils.ts outputs prices with the ₹ symbol instead of $ or any other currency symbol.
Prices on the Product Catalog page (ProductCard.tsx) display in ₹ format.
Prices on the Product Detail page display in ₹ format.
Unit price and line totals on the Cart page display in ₹ format.
Order summary prices and the subtotal/total on the Checkout page display in ₹ format.
Product prices in the Admin Panel table/card list display in ₹ format.
No page in the app shows a $ or other non-INR currency symbol for any price.

file_operations.description: Replace the existing formatPrice function to use Indian Rupee (₹) formatting. Change the Intl.NumberFormat locale from 'en-US' and currency from 'USD' to 'en-IN' locale and 'INR' currency. This will automatically format all prices with the ₹ symbol and Indian number formatting conventions (e.g., ₹1,499.00). Since all components (ProductCard, ProductDetail, Cart, Checkout, Admin) already call this utility function, this single change will cascade the INR format across the entire application.
